rationale: The 2006 paper cites Baumgartner and Steenkamp (2001) at key points where it motivates its expanded error taxonomy, specifically to justify that acquiescence/response styles can create correlated item-subset error and scale-wide error. These are not just list citations; they support the core argument that systematic nonrandom measurement error must be modeled explicitly. Thus, the cited paper functions as conceptual and empirical grounding for one of the central pillars of the proposed measurement paradigm.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the 2001 response-styles study as prior evidence that response styles (especially acquiescence) induce structured measurement error across items and scales. That evidence underpins the citing paper's framework for modeling stable/transient and item-subset/scale-wide error components.
